messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).

No. The Republic is attributed to Plato, not Socrates. This is a common and important distinction to make carefully. Socrates appears as the central character and main speaker in the Republic, which can create the impression that it is his work — but Socrates left no extant writings of his own. Everything we know of his thought comes through the writings of others, primarily Plato and Xenophon. Plato composed the Republic as a philosophical dialogue in which Socrates serves as the voice of the argument, but the authorship belongs to Plato. The Socratic corpus has no surviving first-hand texts.
